transcript: My name is Robert Lewis, and I'm calling today because your Capital One credit card company...

...would like to show their appreciation to you as a valued member, and send you a three month review period of up to one million dollars of accidental insurance at no cost to you. And sir, this is just our way of saying thank you, and to introduce you to the coverage...
No, this is just accidental death insurance, sir.

And... also, just before the three month period is over, they will send you a reminder asking you if you would like to continue with it or not, and if you choose to continue with it, the premium is only seven dollars and ninety-five cents a month. This will never increase at any time...
With a one million dollar one-time charge?
It's one million dollars per accident or...
Yeah, a month. Seven ninety dollars a month.
And this will never increase. And if you choose not to continue with it, there is a 1-800 number that you can call at any time to cancel...
...and you will owe nothing for the plan.
And your middle initial is V, sir?
Uh... so, unless I die, it's not--
You don't really have to die just to get it, sir.
Oh, you... there's a dismemberment clause in there, too.
...like, if you lose eyesight or something, or if you lose a limb.
Wait, are you telling me I'm in some kind of danger here, partner?
No, sir. It's just accidental death insurance. It's something you can review in your own home while being covered for it also, for three full months.
I'll tell you who's gonna be dismembered.
But I don't think you're gonna like it. [exhales]
No, I don't... I don't wish to get the coverage.
But if I'm dismembered, I'll give you a holler.
